case_title: MARCO T. MEZA ET AL VS. PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC. ET AL

docket_entries.description: REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE PLAINTIFFS REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE (TRANSACTION ID # 64651344) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MEZA, MARCO T. AN INDIVIDUAL SILVA, CONCEPCION AN INDIVIDUAL
OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 64651420) FILED BY PLAINTIFF MEZA, MARCO T. AN INDIVIDUAL SILVA, CONCEPCION AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALAN WALDMAN, PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANTS PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC. AND ALAN WALDMAN'S DEMURRERS TO THE INTENTIONAL AND NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS CLAIMS ARE OVERRULED. THE CLAIMS ARE PLEADED WITH THE REQUISITE SPECIFICITY. (AMENDED COMPLAINT, 41-46.) THE CITED REPRESENTATIONS ARE ACTIONABLE EVEN IF THEY ARE CHARACTERIZED AS "OPINION" BECAUSE DEFENDANTS HAD SUPERIOR KNOWLEDGE ON THE TOPIC. (SEE JOLLEY V. CHASE HOME FINANCE, LLC (2013) 213 CAL.APP.4TH 872, 892 ["IT IS WELL SETTLED THAT AN OPINION MAY BE ACTIONABLE WHEN IT IS MADE BY A PARTY WHO 'POSSESS[ES] SUPERIOR KNOWLEDGE.'"]; WALKER V. SIGNAL COMPANIES, INC. (1978) 84 CAL.APP.3D 982, 994 [FRAUD CLAIM BASED ON DEFENDANTS' INITIAL FRAUDULENT INTENTION NOT TO PERFORM WITHIN THE TIME SPECIFIED IN THE CONTRACT]; AMENDED COMPLAINT, 46.)(SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) IN ADDITION, DEFENDANT HAS 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT)ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS)JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ALAN WALDMAN, PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.DEFENDANTS PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC. AND ALAN WALDMAN'S MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. AS NOTED IN THE RULING ON THE DEMURRER, PLAINTIFFS' CLAIMS ARE WELL-PLEADED. PLAINTIFFS ALLEGE SUFFICIENT FACTS TO SUPPORT THE PRAYER FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

LAW AND MOTION DEPT. 302: PLAINTIFFS MARCO MEZA, CONCEPCION SILVA'S MOTION FOR DECREE RELEASING PROPERTY FROM MECHANICS LIEN, TO RELEASE THE APRIL 26, 2019 LIEN IS MOOT, PURSUANT TO MR. WALDMAN'S REPRESENTATION THAT ON NOVEMBER 30, 2020, HE FILED AND RECORDED A RELEASE OF THE LIEN. PLAINTIFF IS AWARDED $3,164.95 IN ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS, PURSUANT TO CIVIL CODE SECTION 8488(C) WHICH ENTITLES A PREVAILING PARTY "TO REASONABLE ATTORNEY'S FEES." A PLAINTIFF MAY "PREVAIL" FOR PURPOSES OF THE CIVIL CODE "IF THE LAWSUIT WAS THE CATALYST MOTIVATING THE DEFENDANT TO MODIFY ITS BEHAVIOR OR THE PLAINTIFF ACHIEVED THE PRIMARY RELIEF SOUGHT." (SEE MOLSKI V. ARCIERO WINE GROUP (2008) 164 CAL.APP.4TH 786, 790.) WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR NO. 8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Decree Releasing Property From Mechanics Lien
Off Calendar Dec-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via ZOOM Video-conferencing:
Attorney, Mark K. Langis of the law offices of LUCAS VALLEY LAW (Tel: 415.472.3892; Email: mdelangis@lucasvalleylaw.com) for Plaintiffs Marco T. Meza and Concepcion Silva

Represented defendant/cross-complainant, Alan Mark Waldman appears makes a late special appearance on his own behalf.

The Court hears from both sides. Defendant/cross-complainant Alan Mark Waldberg, having had some technical difficulties addresses the court outside of the presence of plaintiff counsel, Mr. Delangis, who left the videoconference after the ruling.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

PLAINTIFFS MARCO MEZA, CONCEPCION SILVA'S Motion For Decree Releasing Property From Mechanics Lien, to release the April 26, 2019 lien is MOOT, pursuant to Mr. Waldman's representation that on November 30, 2020, he filed and recorded a release of the lien. Plaintiff is awarded $3,164.95 in attorney's fees and costs, pursuant to Civil Code section 8488(c) which entitles a prevailing party "to reasonable attorney's fees." A plaintiff may "prevail" for purposes of the Civil Code "if the lawsuit was the catalyst motivating the defendant to modify its behavior or the plaintiff achieved the primary relief sought." (See Molski v. Arciero Wine Group (2008) 164 Cal.App.4th 786, 790.)

The Court notes that Mr. Waldman may not represent Pacific Engineering in court. "A corporation cannot represent itself in court, either in propria persona or through an officer or agent who is not an attorney." (Merco Constr. Engineers, Inc. v. Municipal Court (1978) 21 Cal.3d 724, 729.) Defendants have counsel of record, and it is unclear why Mr. Waldman is filing in pro per. Counsel plaintiff is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the adopted ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Written order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark de Langis, Esq. 415-472-3892 for Plaintiff.
James Choo, Esq. 707-789-6500 of O'Brien Law P.C. for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues Plaintiff's misrepresentation and punitive damages. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALAN WALDMAN, PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s Motion To Strike Portions Of The First Amended Complaint.

Defendants Pacific Engineering and Construction, Inc. and Alan Waldman's motion to strike is denied. As noted in the ruling on the demurrer, plaintiffs' claims are well-pleaded. Plaintiffs allege sufficient facts to support the prayer for punitive damages.

Prevailing party to prepare a proposed form of order.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Mark de Langis, Esq. 415-472-3892 for Plaintiff.
James Choo, Esq. 707-789-6500 of O'Brien Law P.C. for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues Plaintiff's misrepresentation and punitive damages. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT ALAN WALDMAN, PACIFIC ENGINEERING AND CONSTRUCTION, INC.'s DEMURRER TO THE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendants Pacific Engineering and Construction, Inc. and Alan Waldman's demurrers to the intentional and negligent misrepresentations claims are overruled. The claims are pleaded with the requisite specificity. (Amended Complaint, 41-46.) The cited representations are actionable even if they are characterized as "opinion" because defendants had superior knowledge on the topic. (See Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 872, 892 ["it is well settled that an opinion may be actionable when it is made by a party who 'possess[es] superior knowledge.'"]; Walker v. Signal Companies, Inc. (1978) 84 Cal.App.3d 982, 994 [fraud claim based on defendants' initial fraudulent intention not to perform within the time specified in the contract]; Amended Complaint, 46.)

Mr. Waldman's demurrer to the breach of contract cause of action is overruled. Mr. Waldman is not bound by the contract based on the doctrine of judicial estoppel. The doctrine of judicial estoppel applies where "(1) the same party has taken two positions; (2) the positions were taken in judicial or quasi-judicial administrative proceedings; (3) the party was successful in asserting the first position (i.e., the tribunal adopted the position or accepted it as true); (4) the two positions are totally inconsistent; and (5) the first position was not taken as a result of ignorance, fraud, or mistake." (Jackson v. County of Los Angeles (1997) 60 Cal.App.4th 171, 183.) Mr. Waldman was not successful in the small claims action as plaintiffs admit that his matter was dismissed. (Amended Complaint, 11.) Based on the contract attached to the complaint, the court is skeptical that Mr. Waldman is a party to the complaint. The corporation was the party to the contract. At any rate, 12 of the complaint alleges that Mr. Waldman "ratified" the contract per Civil Code 1588 and a factual issue remains whether Mr. Waldman in fact agreed to be personally bound by the terms of the agreement.

In addition, Defendant has 10 days to answer. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
